description: Pre-written content queue - NO API credits needed! Add more using Claude Code sessions.

daily_tips.content: Keep your bedroom at 65-68F (18-20C) for optimal sleep. Your body temperature needs to drop for quality rest.
Stop caffeine 8-10 hours before bed. That afternoon coffee might be why you can't fall asleep!
Blue light from screens suppresses melatonin. Use night mode or stop screens 1 hour before bed.
Exercise improves sleep quality, but finish workouts 3-4 hours before bed to avoid being too energized.
Consistent sleep schedule is key! Go to bed and wake up at the same time, even on weekends.
Weighted blankets (7-12% of body weight) can reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality through deep pressure therapy.
White noise masks disruptive sounds and creates a consistent audio environment for better sleep.
Avoid large meals 2-3 hours before bed. Digestion can disrupt your sleep quality.
Magnesium glycinate helps relax muscles and calm the nervous system. Take 200-400mg 1 hour before bed.
Your mattress matters! Replace every 7-10 years. A sagging mattress can cause back pain and poor sleep.
The 4-7-8 breathing technique: Inhale 4 sec, hold 7 sec, exhale 8 sec. Repeat 4x to calm your nervous system.
Alcohol may help you fall asleep but disrupts REM sleep. Avoid drinking 3+ hours before bed.
Complete darkness is essential. Even small lights from electronics can disrupt melatonin production.
Can't sleep after 20 minutes? Get up and do something relaxing until you feel sleepy. Don't toss and turn.
Lavender essential oil has been shown to improve sleep quality. Try a few drops on your pillow.
Morning sunlight exposure helps set your circadian rhythm. Get 10-30 min of natural light after waking.
Your pillow height matters! Side sleepers need higher pillows, back sleepers need medium, stomach sleepers need flat.
Naps are best kept under 20 minutes to avoid sleep inertia. The ideal nap time is between 1-3 PM.
A warm bath 1-2 hours before bed helps. As your body cools down afterward, it triggers sleepiness.
Reserve your bed for sleep only. Working or watching TV in bed weakens the mental association with rest.
Chamomile tea contains apigenin, which binds to receptors in your brain that promote sleepiness.

instagram_captions.caption: Did you know the ideal bedroom temperature for sleep is 65-68F (18-20C)? Your body needs to cool down to fall asleep! Try lowering your thermostat tonight and see the difference.
That 3pm coffee might be ruining your sleep! Caffeine has a half-life of 5-6 hours, meaning half is still in your system. Cut off caffeine by 2pm for better rest tonight.
Weighted blankets aren't just cozy - they're science! Deep pressure stimulation triggers your parasympathetic nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting calm. Game changer for anxious sleepers!
Why does white noise help you sleep? It masks sudden noises that would otherwise wake you up. Your brain stays in a consistent sound environment all night. Try it if you're a light sleeper!
Magnesium is the relaxation mineral! It helps regulate neurotransmitters that calm your nervous system. Most people are deficient. Try magnesium glycinate 1 hour before bed.

Even tiny amounts of light can disrupt your melatonin production. A quality sleep mask creates complete darkness, signaling your brain it's time for deep rest. Especially helpful if you live in a city!
Wrong pillow = neck pain + poor sleep. Side sleepers need firm, high pillows. Back sleepers need medium. Stomach sleepers need thin. What's your sleep position?
Screens before bed suppress melatonin by up to 50%! Blue light blocking glasses can help if you can't avoid screens. Put them on 2 hours before bed for best results.
You can't improve what you don't measure! Sleep trackers show your sleep stages, duration, and quality. Data helps you identify what's helping or hurting your sleep.
Street lights, car headlights, early morning sun - all enemies of deep sleep! Blackout curtains block 99% of light, helping your body produce more melatonin naturally.
Lavender isn't just a nice smell - studies show it can lower heart rate and blood pressure, creating the perfect conditions for sleep. Try a diffuser with lavender essential oil!
The secret to better sleep starts in the MORNING! 10-30 minutes of natural sunlight after waking sets your circadian rhythm, making it easier to fall asleep at night.

engagement_messages.message: Check your Pinterest analytics - which pins got the most saves this week? Create more content like your top performers!
Reply to any comments on your Instagram posts from the last 24 hours. Engagement boosts your visibility!
Save 5-10 pins from other sleep/wellness accounts to your boards. This helps Pinterest show your content to similar audiences.
Check Amazon affiliate dashboard - any sales or clicks this week? Note which products are converting.
Research trending sleep topics on Pinterest. What questions are people asking? Create content that answers them!
Update your Pinterest profile and board descriptions with relevant keywords for better SEO.
Check your Instagram insights - what time are your followers most active? Schedule posts accordingly.
Join 2-3 Pinterest group boards in the sleep/wellness niche. Share your pins there for more reach.
Create a new Pinterest board for a specific topic (e.g., 'Sleep Products for Anxiety'). Niche boards attract targeted followers.
Review your top 5 Amazon affiliate links. Are the products still available and well-reviewed? Update if needed.
Follow 10 accounts in your niche on Instagram. Genuine engagement often leads to follow-backs and collaboration opportunities.
Create a poll or question in your Instagram story to boost engagement and learn what your audience wants.
Check which of your pins are ranking in Pinterest search. Optimize titles and descriptions for those doing well.
Repurpose your best Instagram caption into a Pinterest pin. Same content, different platform, more reach!

weekly_tasks.details: Aim for 5-7 new pins. Use Canva templates. Focus on sleep tips and product recommendations.
Design 2-3 posts for the week. Use consistent branding. Include calls to action.
Check Pinterest, Instagram, and Amazon affiliate dashboards. Note what's working and what's not.
Spend 15-30 min engaging with others in your niche. Comment, save, and share quality content.
Research trending topics, competitor content, and seasonal opportunities for next week.
Queue up any remaining posts for the weekend. Check all links are working.
Review week's performance. Plan content strategy for next week. Use Claude Code to generate new queue content!
